message:
fix(security): sync KNOWN_INSTALLERS URLs from checksums.yaml at load time

When load_checksums() parses the freshly-downloaded checksums.yaml, it now
also extracts the url: field and overrides KNOWN_INSTALLERS. This prevents
checksum mismatches caused by stale URLs baked into an older deployed
security.sh (e.g., after the mcp_agent_mail Python→Rust migration changed
the installer URL from mcp_agent_mail/main/scripts/install.sh to
mcp_agent_mail_rust/main/install.sh).

Previously, refresh_checksums() updated the checksums but KNOWN_INSTALLERS
kept the old URL, causing verify_checksum() to download from the wrong URL
and fail with a mismatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -674,6 +674,13 @@ load_checksums() {
             fi
         fi
 
+        # Match url value for the current tool — override KNOWN_INSTALLERS so
+        # stale URLs baked into an older security.sh are corrected when
+        # checksums.yaml is refreshed from GitHub.
+        if [[ -n "$current_tool" ]] && [[ "$line" =~ url:[[:space:]]*\"(https://[^\"]+)\" ]]; then
+            KNOWN_INSTALLERS["$current_tool"]="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
+        fi
+
         # Match sha256 value for the current tool.
         if [[ -n "$current_tool" ]] && [[ "$line" =~ sha256:[[:space:]]*['\"]?([0-9A-Fa-f]{64})['\"]? ]]; then
             LOADED_CHECKSUMS["$current_tool"]="${BASH_REMATCH[1],,}"
